leg: goal 064 leg B (test-designer) — Germany Bundestag fixture capture via the browser-engine seam
purpose: Establish the DE canonical raw-byte pins the SAF §DE.8 deferred (www.bundestag.de is Enodia/Radware bot-gated; §DE.2). EU + FR fixtures reuse the byte-exact 063a archives (no DE-style capture needed) — this log covers ONLY the DE browser-seam retrievals this leg.

access_method.engine: Playwright 1.61.1 chromium (ms-playwright/chromium-1228/chrome-win64/chrome.exe, HeadlessChrome/149.0.7827.55) — the us_senate §2.5 / australia_register 063b browser-engine seam.
access_method.enodia_gate: www.bundestag.de returns HTTP 400 /.enodia/challenge (a JS proof-of-work + cookie challenge) to EVERY non-browser client. A real headless Chromium executes the challenge automatically: observed 400 /.enodia/challenge?redirect=... then 200 <path>?enodia=<jwt-like-token>. The 'Veröffentlichungspflichtige Angaben' disclosure block is SERVER-RENDERED into the raw main-document response body (confirmed: rawHasDisc=true on every capture). NO fingerprint evasion — the browser's own HeadlessChrome UA is kept (a bot/identified UA trips Enodia); a From: contact header identifies us.
access_method.pin_basis: sha256 of the raw main-document response bytes (Playwright response.body(), NOT page.content() DOM re-serialization — fixture-capture skill). The bytes carry per-session enodia tokens in embedded links so they are NOT byte-reproducible on re-fetch, but the committed fixtures/de_*/input.html is byte-stable (== the pin) and the disclosure DATA is deterministic. This mirrors the australia_register precedent (bot-gated => the archived capture, not a canonically-reproducible source byte-stream, is the pin).

politeness.user_agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) HeadlessChrome/149.0.7827.55 Safari/537.36 (+ From: ssm.leo@outlook.com on www.bundestag.de)
politeness.from_header_scope: route-scoped to **://www.bundestag.de/** only — From is not CORS-safelisted; a context-global header would force preflights that silently kill CDN JS on third-party hosts (fixture-capture skill learning).

discovery: GET /abgeordnete/biografien (index warmup, establishes the Enodia session)
GET /ajax/filterlist/de/abgeordnete/biografien/1040594-1040594?limit=12&offset=0..636 (paginated member enumeration; 636 MdB of the 21. Wahlperiode harvested, ~54 ajax calls @0.6s)
requests.probe_batch: 10 candidate MdB pages fetched @2.5s to locate members with published income/Beteiligungen: willsch, merz, luczak, throm, middelberg, whittaker, linnemann, gutting, stracke, bareiss (all HTTP 200 through the gate). Most 21. WP disclosures list only functions (many ehrenamtlich) with no euro amounts; throm/stracke/gutting carried exact euro figures.

established_pins_note: These 3 sha256 ESTABLISH the DE raw-byte pins the SAF §DE.8 recorded as '_capture leg_'. The SAF methodology page is founder/methodology-gated, so it was NOT edited here; fixtures/MANIFEST.json is the source of truth for the DE pins (builder/audit leg backfills §DE.8).
disclosure_html_structure: div.e-collapse__content > div.m-biography__infos: a sequence of <h3> category headings (the §DE.3 8-category vocabulary, verbatim) each followed by one or more <ul class="voa_list bt-liste"> ENTRIES. Per entry: first <li> = organisation (name, location); following <li>(s) = role/function [+ published income 'monatlich|jährlich|einmalig, N,NN Euro, Brutto/Gewinn...']; an optional NESTED <ul> = anonymised contract partners ('Mandant N, sector, years'). The build leg's scraper selectors + the value/period parse are specified in fixtures/MANIFEST.json builder_notes.de.
